indicators.name: large freestanding bathtub
indicators.rationale: A large bathtub is not conducive to computer work as it takes up a significant amount of space and is not ergonomically suitable for prolonged use.

indicators.name: floor-to-ceiling windows
indicators.rationale: Large floor-to-ceiling windows can be distracting and may allow unwanted light and noise to enter the space.

indicators.name: no flat personal work surface
indicators.rationale: There is no flat surface at sitting height to set up a computer workstation.

indicators.name: no ergonomic seating
indicators.rationale: There is no ergonomic seating available for prolonged computer use.

indicators.name: no dedicated lighting for computer work
indicators.rationale: There is no task lighting specifically designed for computer work.

reasoning_summary: The indoor scene is marginally adequate for computer work (Solo). The presence of a large freestanding bathtub and large floor-to-ceiling windows make the space less suitable for focused work. There is no flat personal work surface or ergonomic seating, and no dedicated lighting for computer work. However, the presence of a large window could potentially provide natural light, which might be beneficial for some users.
